primary_topics: weld hydrogen levels and electrode classifications
peening (Almen gauge, fatigue, peening as stress relief)
weldability test methods (cruciform test, Tekken test)
delayed cracking and post-weld inspection timing
recurring_cases_mentioned: Simpson Gumpertz and Heger (Boston structural engineers, MIT civil)

key_figures_and_data: 900°F as threshold for steel residual stress relief
30 ppm hydrogen tolerance for 70 ksi steel
4 ppm hydrogen guarantee for extra-low-hydrogen electrodes
100 ppm potential hydrogen in stick electrodes
25°C as minimum-time-to-failure temperature in H2S stress corrosion
1 hour at 375°F for low-strength steel; 23 hours at 375°F for 200 ksi steel
Coefficient of thermal expansion 10^-5/°C as precision-machining floor
Shipbuilding tolerance: 1/4 inch over 100 yards (~1 part in 14,400)
US Navy 7-day wait before testing for hydrogen cracks
AWS D1.1 Structural Welding Code (handout reference)
